steps.title: Identify the contrast signal
steps.content:
The word “whereas” creates a direct logical opposition between two conditions affecting decision-making speed.

The passage establishes that intensified information flow leads to faster, more decisive choices.

The blank must describe a contrasting state that explains why decisions slow down—specifically, the opposite of “intensified.”

steps.title: Match the target meaning
steps.content:
The blank requires the opposite of “intensified.” Reduced expresses this precisely—when information flow is reduced between brain regions, decision-making becomes slower, maintaining the causal logic of the passage.

The other three (evaluated, determined, acquired) do not establish opposition to “intensified” and fail to explain a causal effect on decision speed.

This fulfills the “whereas” structure: more intensified flow → faster decisions; reduced flow → slower decisions.

That is the correct answer.
